themeName: STARで答える行動面接（英語）

items.meaning: 行動面接の回答を Situation→Task→Action→Result で構成する。
items.detail_ja: 「〜した経験は？」型の質問は、状況(Situation)・自分の責務(Task)・取った行動(Action)・結果(Result, 可能なら数値)の順で簡潔に語ると伝わる。Actionを厚く『自分が何をしたか』を、Resultは定量で締める。

pron.tts: I structure behavioral answers with STAR: situation, task, action, and result.

explain.prompt_ja: STARメソッドで行動面接にどう答えるかを英語で説明して。
points_ja: Actionを厚く・自分が何をしたか
key_phrases: as a result / which led to
explain.model_en: I structure behavioral answers with STAR: situation, task, action, and result.
explain.model_en_long: For behavioral questions I use the STAR method. I briefly set the situation and my task, spend most of the time on the specific actions I took, and finish with a measurable result. Keeping the action centered on what I personally did, and quantifying the result, makes the answer clear and convincing.

items.themeName: STARで答える行動面接（英語）

items.front: Describe a project you're proud of
items.meaning: 誇れるプロジェクトをSTARで。役割→技術選択→定量成果。
items.detail_ja: 自分の役割・課題・技術的アクション・定量成果を語る。下の模範はテンプレなので、必ず自分の実体験に置き換えること。
items.note: ※ model_en_long はテンプレ。自分の実プロジェクト・数値に差し替える。

pron.tts: In my last role, I rebuilt a slow, fragile pipeline into reliable Spark jobs.

explain.prompt_ja: 誇れるプロジェクトを英語で（STARで）話して。
points_ja: 自分の技術的アクションを具体的に
定量成果で締める（※自分の実例に差し替え）
key_phrases: as a result, X dropped from ... to ...
explain.model_en: In my last role I rebuilt a slow, fragile nightly pipeline into reliable Spark jobs.
explain.model_en_long: In my last role, our nightly pipeline was too slow and often failed, so my task was to make it reliable and faster. I rewrote it as idempotent Spark jobs, fixed a data-skew problem with salting, and added data-quality tests. As a result, the runtime dropped from four hours to forty minutes and the on-call alerts almost disappeared.

items.themeName: STARで答える行動面接（英語）

items.meaning: 技術的に難しかった課題と解決をSTARで。
items.detail_ja: 根本原因をどう特定し、どう直したかを具体的に。下はテンプレなので自分の事例に。
items.note: ※ テンプレ。自分が実際に直した障害・原因・結果に置き換える。

pron.tts: The hardest problem was a job that kept failing at two a.m.

explain.prompt_ja: 直面した難しい技術課題と、その解決を英語で（STARで）話して。
points_ja: 症状→調査→根本原因→対処の順
自分の分析・判断を具体的に
安定/高速化など結果（※自分の事例に）
key_phrases: the root cause turned out to be
explain.model_en: The hardest problem I solved was a job that kept failing at two a.m. with no clear cause.
explain.model_en_long: We had a job that kept failing at 2 a.m. and nobody knew why, so my task was to find the root cause. I added structured logging and traced it to a skewed join on one huge customer. I fixed it by broadcasting the small side and salting the key. As a result the job became stable and ran about five times faster.

items.themeName: STARで答える行動面接（英語）

items.meaning: トレードオフのある意思決定の語り方。
items.detail_ja: 選択肢・比較軸・根拠・結果を語る。判断の合理性とコミュニケーションを見られる。下はテンプレ。
items.note: ※ テンプレ。自分が実際に下した技術判断に置き換える。

pron.tts: We had to choose between a Lambda and a simpler Kappa architecture.

explain.prompt_ja: トレードオフのある技術判断を英語で（STARで）話して。
points_ja: 選択肢と比較軸を明示
結果＋判断を共有したこと（※自分の事例に）
explain.model_en: We had to choose between a Lambda architecture and a simpler streaming-only design.
explain.model_en_long: We had to choose between a Lambda architecture and a simpler streaming-only Kappa design, and my task was to recommend one. I weighed freshness against the cost of maintaining two code paths, prototyped both, and recommended Kappa because our latency needs were modest. As a result we cut maintenance and shipped faster, and I documented the trade-off so the team understood the decision.

items.themeName: STARで答える行動面接（英語）

items.meaning: 失敗と学びを前向きに語る。
items.detail_ja: 責任を認めつつ、再発防止と学びに着地させる。下はテンプレ。
items.note: ※ テンプレ。自分の実際の失敗・学びに置き換える。

pron.tts: Early on, I broke a dashboard by deploying a schema change without a contract.

explain.prompt_ja: 失敗経験と学びを英語で（STARで）前向きに話して。
points_ja: どう対処・再発防止したか
学びで締める（※自分の事例に）
key_phrases: to prevent it from happening again
explain.model_en: Early on, I broke a dashboard by deploying a schema change without a data contract.
explain.model_en_long: Early on, I deployed a schema change without a data contract and broke a downstream dashboard during business hours. My task was to fix it fast and prevent a repeat, so I rolled back, communicated clearly with the affected team, and then introduced data contracts and CI tests. As a result that class of failure stopped, and I learned to design for the consumers of my data, not just the producers.

items.themeName: STARで答える行動面接（英語）

items.front: Why this role / moving to Australia
items.meaning: 志望動機と移住理由を、前向き・誠実・長期志向で。
items.detail_ja: 役割への具体的関心＋自分の強みが活きる点＋長期で貢献・成長したい意欲を簡潔に。移住は前向きな理由で。下はテンプレ。
items.note: ※ テンプレ。自分の言葉・本当の動機に調整する。

pron.tts: I'm moving to Australia for the long term and want to grow and contribute here.

explain.prompt_ja: この職種への志望動機とオーストラリア移住の理由を英語で話して。
points_ja: 役割への関心を具体的に
自分の強みが活きる点
長期・前向きな移住理由（※自分の言葉に）
key_phrases: what draws me to this role
I want to grow and contribute
explain.model_en: What draws me to this role is building reliable, well-modeled data platforms at scale.
explain.model_en_long: I'm a data engineer who enjoys turning messy data into reliable, well-modeled platforms, and this role focuses on exactly that at a scale I'm excited by. I'm moving to Australia for the long term—I want to grow here, contribute to the team, and keep improving my English in a real working environment. I see it as a place to build a stable career, not a short stay.

items.themeName: STARで答える行動面接（英語）
